title: Dark Chocolate Crème Brûlée
instructions: Bring cream and half-and-half to a boil in a heavy saucepan.
Reduce heat to low, add chocolate, and whisk until melted and smooth.
In a bowl, whisk egg yolks with ⅓ cup sugar until blended.
Gradually whisk in hot chocolate mixture.
Place ramekins in a baking pan and add hot water to reach halfway up the sides.
Bake until just set, about 50 minutes, or if using steam oven, cook at steam mode 210 degrees F for 20 minutes.
Remove ramekins from water bath and chill several hours or overnight.
Before serving, sprinkle each custard with about 1 tablespoon sugar.
Broil until sugar melts and turns golden, watching closely.
notes: Steam oven alternative: cook at steam mode 210°F for 20 minutes instead of baking at 300°F for 50 minutes
Can be made ahead and chilled several hours or overnight before finishing with the brûlée step
Watch closely while broiling to prevent burning the sugar
